name: restart-service-rolling
description: Rolling restart of a GKE deployment with zero downtime. Safe for all services and the default first-line remediation for stale-state or transient-failure incidents.

name: restart-service-with-memory-bump
description: Rolling restart that also raises the Kubernetes memory limit for the deployment. The canonical fix for OOM-kill incidents during promotional events, especially on checkout-service.

name: scale-up-replicas
description: Increase HPA min replicas for a deployment. Safe for stateless services, used during promotional traffic spikes or when latency is climbing but pods are healthy.

name: rollback-to-previous-version
description: Roll a GKE deployment back to the previous Harness artifact version. Used when a recent deploy introduced the failure mode and rolling forward would be slower than reverting.

name: clear-redis-cache
description: Flush a named Redis cache. Used when checkout sessions are bloated or product-search result cache is thrashing after a pricing-engine bulk update.

name: reindex-elasticsearch
description: Full reindex of the product-search Elasticsearch index from the canonical catalog. Takes approximately 40 minutes; during the rebuild, search returns slightly stale results — this is expected.

name: force-inventory-sync
description: Trigger a manual inventory-sync run against the IBM AS/400 warehouse system, with strict record-count validation enabled. Standard fix for the silent zero-record write failure mode.

name: drain-and-cordon-node
description: Drain and cordon a GKE node so the scheduler rebalances pods off it. High risk because it removes a node from service; requires human review and a high confidence floor.

name: rotate-service-credentials
description: Rotate OAuth tokens and Vault-managed credentials for a service. Restricted to payment-processor by default because of PCI scope; high risk and high confidence floor.

name: trigger-manual-fulfilment-retry
description: Replay queued order-fulfilment dispatches to a specific partner. NEVER automatic — duplicate physical shipments have occurred twice historically. Always human-reviewed.
